displayTitle: Ask the Part, Prove the Numbers

beats.narration: Current, field direction, particle system: magnetic particle testing is full of knobs. Skilled technicians improvising run two different tests. The written procedure closes that gap.

payload.title: WRITE THE TEST
payload.sub: How a procedure is developed — and proven before it's trusted.

beats.narration: Development starts with the part. It asks five questions: history, manufacturing process, possible defect causes, usage, and acceptance criteria.
Procedures state windows, not points: amperage commonly plus or minus ten percent, field window thirty to sixty gauss, prod spacing six to eight inches.
Validation: tape Q Q I shims, notch side toward the part, at worst-case spots. Blooming notches plus an in-window Hall reading prove the field adequate.

payload.heading: Prove it at the worst-case spots
payload.note: NOTCH SIDE TO PART · BLOOM = ADEQUATE FIELD

beats.narration: Every number in the procedure earns its place: calculated, validated, controlled. Change the qualified system, and you validate again.

payload.sub: Change the qualified system — validate again.
